description: When corrective measures are taken, they must be carried out in accordance with the energy efficiency requirements.
The Automation and Control System shall be installed above 290 kW in heating and air-conditioning systems by 2025 and in renovated buildings from 2021.

System requirements will be set for technical building systems in terms of overall energy performance, correct installation and appropriate dimensioning, adjustment and control. The requirements concern technical systems that replace and upgrade existing systems in the building and shall be applied in so far as they are technically, economically and functionally feasible (Government proposal due in 2020).
Rental housing companies, real estate companies and municipalities linked to energy performance contracts for their premises implement the energy savings targets for the contract period until 2025.
The use of coal for energy production will end by 2029. EUR 90 million will support energy producers phasing out coal by 2025.
Part of fossil fuel oil must be replaced by bio-oil.
Phasing out fossil fuels for individual heating by 2050 by setting a requirement for the share of renewable energy.
The report of the Ministry of Employment and the Economy's Energy Efficiency Working Group has been completed and the implementation of the measures.
Legislation on the assessment of LCA repair projects.

measure_name: Energy efficiency in maintenance and renovation projects
description: Staged deep renovations would require changes to the existing building permit procedure (validity of the permit, plan for energy efficiency improvements).

measure_name: Weakest part of the building stock
description: Housing companies have been enabled to demolish the building.
Billing of warm water based on metered consumption.
Municipalities that have signed energy performance contracts and other public property owners will implement energy saving measures for the contract period up to 2025.
status: Implemented - Energy performance contracts period 2017-2015 and 2026-2035

description: The central government will phase out oil for heating by 2025 and encourage other public actors to do the same.
Consolidate low-carbon to cover: in addition to heating energy consumption, the whole life cycle of the building (LCA perspective).
status: Implemented by the Construction Act Finlex 751/2023

description: Definition of the functional characteristics of smart meters and installation of generation II electricity meters.
status: Implemented - 2025 will be switched to electricity measurement for 15 minutes

description: Legislative changes (e.g. legislation on energy communities).
Buildings with intelligent automation systems when systems are being replaced.
Central information exchange system for retail electricity markets.
Ensuring the interoperability of built environment data through cooperation and legislation.
Those undertaking a construction project shall ensure that the building is designed and constructed in such a way as to ensure fire safety for the purpose.

The load-bearing structures of a building shall be such that they can withstand a minimum period of time in the event of a fire, taking into account the collapse of the building, securing the exit, rescue and recovery of the fire.
It must be possible to limit the development and spread of fire and smoke in the building and the spread of fire to neighbouring buildings.
Construction products and technical installations suitable for fire safety shall be used in the construction of the building.
Oil heating of state and municipal properties to be phased out by 2024.
Encouraging oil-fired buildings to switch to other forms of heating during the 2020s.

measure_name: The weakest part of the building stock
description: Energy grant for improvements beyond the standard of residential buildings.

measure_name: The weakest part of the building stock
description: A renovation allowance for the condition studies of damp/microbial/indoor housing and residential buildings and for the planning costs of renovations of such buildings.

measure_name: The weakest part of the building stock
description: Assistance for the demolition of unoccupied and/or particularly degraded buildings.
The energy subsidy allocated to residential buildings in 2020-2022 can be used for planning costs.
Explore possibilities to support building stock development and energy efficiency improvements in areas where access to market-based financing is problematic.
Vulnerable households can apply for housing assistance for housing maintenance costs, including water and heating costs.
Vulnerable households can claim income support for basic expenditure such as rent or consideration for their own home, heating costs for a single-family house, water charges or household electricity.
Grant for financial advisory activities for tenants 2018-2021.
Renovation allowances for individuals for the renovation of elderly and disabled housing.
Grant to individuals for building condition assessment and renovation planning.
Grant to housing companies for fitness research and renovation planning.
Energy efficiency measures have the possibility to apply for energy support.
Energy aid for ESCO services.
Continue voluntary energy and renewable energy audits of municipalities.

The subsidy conditions of ARA's subsidised loans for multi-dwelling residential buildings will be amended to make them a more attractive option for sites that do not receive market-based financing for renovation projects.
As short-term cyclical public subsidies disrupt markets and temporarily increase prices, financial incentives need to be long-term and predictable.
Continuation of the energy grant for residential buildings after 2022.
Public support and incentives will be directed in particular towards energy efficiency improvements beyond standards and testing of new technologies and concepts.
status: Recommendation/Implemented energy support for new technologies - Finlex 262/2023

description: Residential energy subsidy for energy renovation of residential buildings beyond the level required by the energy regulations for the years 2020-2022.
Increased household allowance for those giving up oil heating fixed-term 2022-2027.
Interest-subsidy loan for renovation of rented and right-of-occupancy buildings for an indefinite period.
Guarantee loans for the renovation of housing companies buildings for an indefinite period.
Repair allowances for the renovation of housing for the elderly and persons with disabilities.

Energy support to companies and communities for investments in conventional technologies for those who have signed an energy performance contract. Increased support for ESCO projects. In force from 2017 to 2025.
Energy support to companies and communities for renewable energy investments. To be decided annually.
Energy support for demonstration projects for innovative solutions contributing to the transition to a low-carbon energy system. To be decided annually.

measure_name: Redirecting investments towards energy efficiency improvements in the public building stock
description: In the public sector, ESCO projects are supported and implemented during the energy performance contracting period (2017-2025).

Explore an impact investment model where the renovation investment is financed by private investors and housing companies and a performance reward is paid to investors according to the achievement of the performance targets set for energy savings and emission reductions.
The revision of the Energy Certificates Database will take into account the information needs of the financial sector.
Companies providing energy renovation services provide financial institutions with secured information on the energy and cost savings that can be achieved by products and services.
Enhance advice on green financing opportunities for renovation projects.
Seek to make sustainable funding available, including in declining areas, for buildings with long-term prospects for use.
Continue and intensify cooperation in the real estate construction and financial sectors.

Explore ways to address identified skills and labour shortages, e.g. by increasing training especially in polytechnics and by developing the content of automation training for vocational learners and polytechnics.
The established competence centre for innovative public procurement KEINO which has issued procurement guidelines covering carbon neutrality targets resource wisdom and the circular economy.

Undergraduate studies in the construction sector at all levels of education include the teaching of building physics, building materials, production techniques, building management, house engineering etc. These criteria must also be taught from other sectors to students undergoing training.
Increase the open digital education offer for continuous learning in renovation and energy efficiency.

status: Implemented - Centre for Continuous Learning (JOTPA) Finlex 682/2021

Improving the competence, reliability and value of maintenance as a whole.
Continuous learning pathway in mathematics from nursery school through primary and secondary school to vocational and higher education.
Efforts will be made to make energy class information mandatory for advertisements on housing sales.

Easily accessible and transparent information on successful cost-effective measures to assist with advice.
One-stop-shop for renovation to provide consumers with information on energy renovations, funding and grants, service providers, support for aggregation of projects (group renovation).

Information for the market and the customer on the impact of the energy efficiency investment on the value and operating costs of the property over its life cycle (taxpayer, insurance company, tenants, owner).
